title: Label Properties
description:
Label Properties

Label Properties and Events

The following list is a summary of the Label control properties with brief descriptions.  Click on the property names for more details.

Property	Description

BackColor	Selects the background color for the
		 object's label.

Default: Control

BorderStyle	Sets the border style.

Default: 0 -
		 None

Enabled	Determines if the control is enabled
		 or not.

Default: 1 -
		 True

EventTaskType	The type of task used for events.

Default: 0 -
		 Normal

Font	The font for the control text (designtime
		 only).

Default: Microsoft
		 Sans Serif, 8.25pt.

FontBold	The bold attribute for the current font
		 (Only available from SPEL+ programs).

Default: 0 - False

FontItalic	The italic attribute for the current
		 font (Only available from SPEL+ programs).

Default: 0 - False

FontName	The name for the current font (Only
		 available from SPEL+ programs).

Default: Microsoft Sans Serif

FontSize	The size for the current font in points
		 (Only available from SPEL+ programs).

Default: 8.25

ForeColor	Selects the forecolor for the control.

Default: ControlText

Height	The height of the control in pixels.

Image	Sets an image for the control.

Default: None

ImageAlign	Determines the alignment for an image
		 in the control.

Default: 5 -
		 MiddleCenter

Left	The x coordinate
		 of the control in pixels.

Name	Used to assign a unique name to the
		 Label control.

Default: LabelXX

TabIndex	Sets the tab index for the control

Default: 0

Text	Sets the text for the label control.

Default:

TextAlign	Determines alignment for the text.

Default: 1 -
		 TopLeft

ToolTipText	Sets the text
		 for the tooltip.

Default:

Top	Sets the location of the top of the
		 label control.

Visible	Sets if the
		 control is Visible or not.

Default: 1 -
		 True

Width	Sets the width
		 of the label control.

Event	Description

Click	Executes when the user clicks the control
		 with the mouse.

DblClick	Executes when the user double clicks
		 the control with the mouse.
